title: EPIC: Pull the Pin Game
description:
# EPIC: Pull the Pin (Physics Puzzle)

## Strategic Context

Pull the Pin introduces physics-based gameplay, differentiating from the pure logic puzzles of other games. This genre is extremely popular in mobile ads, often featuring dramatic gold collection or lava avoidance.

## Why This Game?

1. **Physics Fun:** Real-time ball physics creates emergent gameplay
2. **Visual Drama:** Balls bouncing, lava sizzling, gold collecting
3. **High Shareability:** Physics chaos makes for great GIFs
4. **Pin Timing:** Introduces timing/sequence element
5. **Ad Ubiquity:** "Pull the Pin" is literally a genre name now

## Game Mechanics

### State Representation
```javascript
{
  balls: [
    { id: 1, x: 100, y: 50, vx: 0, vy: 0, radius: 15, color: "gold", collected: false }
  ],
  pins: [
    { id: "pin1", x: 150, y: 100, angle: 0, length: 60, removed: false }
  ],
  hazards: [
    { type: "lava", x: 50, y: 300, width: 100, height: 20 }
  ],
  goals: [
    { x: 150, y: 350, width: 80, height: 40, required: 3, collected: 0 }
  ],
  walls: [
    { type: "segment", x1: 0, y1: 0, x2: 0, y2: 400 },  // Collision geometry
    // ... more walls defining level shape
  ],
  physicsState: "paused",  // "paused" | "running"
  pinsRemaining: 3
}
```

### Core Rules

**Physics Engine (Simplified):**
- Gravity: constant downward acceleration
- Bounce: coefficient of restitution on wall collision
- Friction: velocity decay over time
- No rotation physics (balls don't spin visually)

**Pin Mechanics:**
- Tap pin to remove it
- Pins act as walls when present
- Removal order matters (puzzle element)
- Some levels: pins must be removed in sequence

**Ball Types:**
- Gold: must reach goal
- Bomb: destroys adjacent balls (avoid)
- Multiplier: doubles score (optional collection)

**Hazard Types:**
- Lava: destroys balls on contact
- Spikes: destroys balls on contact
- Teleporter: moves ball to paired exit

**Win Condition:**
- Required number of gold balls reach goal
- No critical balls lost to hazards

**Fail Condition:**
- Too many gold balls destroyed
- (In zen mode: just restart, no penalty)

### Physics Implementation

**No External Dependencies** - custom lightweight engine:

```javascript
class PhysicsEngine {
  constructor() {
    this.gravity = 0.5;
    this.friction = 0.99;
    this.bounce = 0.7;
  }

  update(balls, walls, dt) {
    for (const ball of balls) {
      // Gravity
      ball.vy += this.gravity * dt;
      
      // Friction
      ball.vx *= this.friction;
      ball.vy *= this.friction;
      
      // Position
      ball.x += ball.vx * dt;
      ball.y += ball.vy * dt;
      
      // Wall collisions
      for (const wall of walls) {
        if (this.circleLineIntersect(ball, wall)) {
          this.resolveCollision(ball, wall);
        }
      }
    }
  }

  circleLineIntersect(ball, wall) {
    // Line segment to circle collision detection
    // Returns: { intersects: boolean, normal: {x, y}, penetration: number }
  }

  resolveCollision(ball, wall) {
    // Reflect velocity along normal
    // Apply bounce coefficient
    // Push ball out of penetration
  }
}
```

**Performance Target:**
- 60fps with up to 20 balls
- Substep physics if frame drops
- Use requestAnimationFrame properly

### Visual Design

**Art Direction:**
```css
--ball-gold: linear-gradient(135deg, #FFD700, #FFA500);
--ball-bomb: radial-gradient(circle, #333, #111);
--pin-metal: linear-gradient(90deg, #C0C0C0, #808080, #C0C0C0);
--lava: linear-gradient(0deg, #FF4500, #FF6B00);
--goal: linear-gradient(0deg, #00C853, #00E676);
--wall: #4A4A5A;
```

**Visual Effects:**
- Ball: subtle shine, shadow underneath
- Lava: animated shimmer, particles rising
- Goal: pulsing glow, collection sparkles
- Pin: metallic sheen, removal animation

**Animations:**
| Action | Duration | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| Pin tap | 50ms | Highlight flash |
| Pin remove | 200ms | Slide out + fade |
| Ball bounce | Per impact | Squash/stretch deformation |
| Lava contact | 300ms | Sizzle particles + poof |
| Goal collect | 400ms | Sparkle burst + counter increment |
| Level win | 800ms | All gold shimmer + fanfare |

### Level Generation

**Challenges:**
- Harder than logic puzzles (physics is chaotic)
- Requires simulation to verify solvability
- Manual level design likely more reliable

**Approach:**
1. Handcraft 50+ levels (primary content)
2. Simple generator for infinite mode:
   - Place goal at bottom
   - Place balls at top
   - Add pins/walls to create path
   - Verify via simulation (multiple runs for chaos)
   - Accept if > 80% success rate

## Zen Mode Considerations

Pull the Pin is the **least zen-compatible** game:
- Inherent fail state (balls in lava)
- Timing pressure from physics
- Can frustrate easily

**Partial Zen Adaptations:**
- Remove time-sensitive levels
- Slower physics (0.7x speed)
- More forgiving goal requirements
- No lava in zen levels (walls only)

## Integration Points

**Shared Systems:**
- Standard modules
- Sound system critical (impacts, sizzles)

**Unique Systems:**
- Custom physics engine
- Particle system
- Level editor (future)

## Dependencies

**Requires:**
- Foundation complete
- Any one game validates patterns

**Independent Of:**
- Other games (physics is unique)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Physics feels "right" (playtested)
- [ ] 40 handcrafted levels
- [ ] Pin removal responsive
- [ ] Ball-wall collision accurate
- [ ] Lava/goal detection correct
- [ ] 60fps maintained
- [ ] GIF export captures physics drama
- [ ] Sound design enhances impact

## Estimated Effort

**Total:** 35-45 hours (physics is complex)
**Complexity:** High
**Priority:** P2 (Parallel with other games)

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| Physics bugs | High | Medium | Extensive testing, simple rules |
| Performance issues | Medium | High | Profiling, substep fallback |
| Level design difficulty | Medium | Medium | Focus on handcrafted |
| Zen incompatibility | Certain | Low | Accept limited zen support |

title: Implement Pull the Pin physics engine
description:
# Implement Pull the Pin Physics

## Physics Requirements
- Gravity (constant downward acceleration)
- Ball-wall collision with bounce
- Friction (velocity decay)
- Pin removal (walls become passable)

## Simplified Engine (No Dependencies)
Custom physics for:
- Circle vs line segment collision
- Bounce reflection
- 60fps update loop

## Performance Target
- 60fps with 20+ balls
- Substep on frame drops

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gravity feels natural
- [ ] Collisions accurate
- [ ] Bounce coefficient adjustable
- [ ] Pin removal works
- [ ] 60fps maintained

title: EPIC: Water Sort - First Game
description:
# EPIC: Water Sort - First Game Implementation

## Strategic Context

Water Sort is the flagship game and serves as the template for all subsequent games. This implementation will establish patterns, validate the shared systems, and create reusable components that other games will inherit.

## Why Water Sort First?

1. **Simplest Mechanics:** Tap-to-select, tap-to-pour makes it easy to validate input handling
2. **No Physics:** Pure logic-based, no physics engine required
3. **Visual Appeal:** Liquid animations are satisfying and shareable
4. **Ad Prevalence:** Most common "fake gameplay" ad type - high search volume
5. **Zen Compatible:** Perfect candidate for zen mode validation

## Game Mechanics Deep Dive

### Core Rules

**State Representation:**
```javascript
{
  tubes: [
    ["red", "blue", "red", "green"],  // Bottom to top
    ["blue", "green", "blue", "red"],
    ["green", "red", "green", "blue"],
    [],  // Empty tube
    []   // Empty tube
  ],
  selectedTube: null,
  moves: 0
}
```

**Valid Move Conditions:**
1. Source tube is not empty
2. Destination tube is not full (< 4 segments)
3. Destination is empty OR top color matches source top color

**Pour Behavior:**
- All consecutive same-color segments from top of source pour together
- e.g., ["red", "red", "blue"] → pour moves both reds at once

**Win Condition:**
- All tubes are either:
  - Empty, OR
  - Contain exactly 4 segments of the same color

### Visual Design

**Color Palette (6 colors for initial levels):**
```css
--liquid-red: #FF6B6B;     /* Warm, inviting */
--liquid-blue: #4ECDC4;    /* Teal, calming */
--liquid-green: #95E879;   /* Fresh, vibrant */
--liquid-yellow: #FFE66D;  /* Sunny, cheerful */
--liquid-purple: #A66CFF;  /* Rich, royal */
--liquid-orange: #FF9F45;  /* Energetic */
```

**Tube Design:**
- Glass effect with subtle transparency
- Rounded bottom, open top
- White highlight along left edge
- Subtle shadow underneath
- Capacity: 4 liquid segments

**Animation Choreography:**
| Action | Duration | Easing | Description |
|--------|----------|--------|-------------|
| Select | 150ms | ease-out | Scale 1.05, lift 8px, glow ring |
| Deselect | 100ms | ease-in | Return to normal |
| Pour Start | 200ms | ease-in | Tilt source tube 45° |
| Liquid Arc | 400ms | custom bezier | Liquid follows parabolic path |
| Liquid Fill | 200ms | ease-out | Stack into destination |
| Pour End | 150ms | ease-out | Return source to upright |
| Invalid | 200ms | ease-in-out | Horizontal shake (±5px, 3x) |
| Tube Complete | 400ms | bounce | Sparkle burst, tube "locks" |
| Level Win | 1200ms | staged | Confetti, all tubes sparkle sequentially |

### Level Design Philosophy

**Difficulty Curve:**
- Levels 1-10: 3 colors, 4 tubes (2 empty) - Tutorial difficulty
- Levels 11-30: 4 colors, 6 tubes (2 empty) - Easy
- Levels 31-60: 5 colors, 7 tubes (2 empty) - Medium
- Levels 61-100: 6 colors, 8 tubes (2 empty) - Hard
- Infinite mode: 6-8 colors, scales based on skill

**Level Quality Criteria:**
- Solvable in < 50 moves (verified by solver)
- Multiple solution paths exist (not forced single path)
- No "obvious" first move (some thinking required)
- Aesthetically balanced initial state

### Procedural Generation Algorithm

```javascript
function generateWaterSortLevel(seed, config) {
  const rng = createRNG(seed);
  const { colors, tubes, emptyTubes } = config;

  // Step 1: Create solved state
  const solved = [];
  for (let c = 0; c < colors; c++) {
    solved.push(Array(4).fill(COLORS[c]));
  }
  for (let e = 0; e < emptyTubes; e++) {
    solved.push([]);
  }

  // Step 2: Reverse solve (make valid pours in reverse)
  let state = structuredClone(solved);
  const shuffleMoves = 30 + Math.floor(rng() * 50);
  
  for (let i = 0; i < shuffleMoves; i++) {
    const moves = getValidReverseMoves(state);
    if (moves.length === 0) break;
    const move = moves[Math.floor(rng() * moves.length)];
    applyMove(state, move);
  }

  // Step 3: Verify solvable (BFS, max 100 moves)
  if (!isSolvable(state, 100)) {
    return generateWaterSortLevel(seed + 1, config); // Retry
  }

  return {
    tubes: state,
    seed,
    optimal: findOptimalMoves(state),
    difficulty: calculateDifficulty(state)
  };
}
```

## Integration Points

**Shared Systems Used:**
- `shared/meta.js` - XP awards, level tracking
- `shared/storage.js` - Save progress, best scores
- `shared/daily.js` - Daily challenge seed
- `shared/history.js` - Undo/redo timeline
- `shared/audio.js` - Pour sounds, win fanfare

**Features Enabled:**
- GIF export of solutions
- Infinite mode
- Daily challenges
- Zen mode

## Success Metrics

- Average session length: 10+ minutes
- Level completion rate: > 85%
- GIF export usage: > 5% of completions
- Zero critical bugs at launch

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 50 handcrafted levels playable
- [ ] Procedural generator produces valid levels
- [ ] Touch controls responsive (< 50ms latency)
- [ ] All animations smooth (60fps)
- [ ] Undo/redo works correctly
- [ ] Progress saves between sessions
- [ ] Daily challenge uses correct seed
- [ ] Sound effects play appropriately
- [ ] Colorblind mode distinguishable
- [ ] Works on mobile Safari, Chrome, Firefox

## Estimated Effort

**Total:** 30-40 hours
**Complexity:** Medium-High (template for other games)
**Priority:** P1 (First deliverable)

Create 50 handcrafted Water Sort levels
description:
# Create Water Sort Levels

## Context
Handcrafted levels provide curated difficulty progression.
Procedural generation supplements but doesn't replace these.

## Level Design Guidelines

### Difficulty Tiers
- **Levels 1-10 (Tutorial):** 3 colors, 4 tubes, very easy
- **Levels 11-25 (Easy):** 4 colors, 5-6 tubes
- **Levels 26-40 (Medium):** 5 colors, 6-7 tubes
- **Levels 41-50 (Hard):** 6 colors, 7-8 tubes

### Quality Criteria
Each level must:
1. Be solvable in reasonable moves (verify with solver)
2. Have documented optimal solution
3. Not have "obvious" first move
4. Be aesthetically balanced
5. Offer multiple solution paths

## Level Format (levels.json)
```json
[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "difficulty": 0.1,
    "optimal": 3,
    "tubes": [
      ["red", "red", "blue", "blue"],
      ["blue", "blue", "red", "red"],
      []
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "difficulty": 0.15,
    "optimal": 5,
    "tubes": [
      ["red", "blue", "red", "blue"],
      ["blue", "red", "blue", "red"],
      [],
      []
    ]
  }
  // ... 48 more levels
]
```

## Level Creation Process
1. Design level visually
2. Verify solvable with solver
3. Record optimal move count
4. Assign difficulty rating (0-1)
5. Playtest for "fun factor"

## Tools Needed
- Solver script to verify levels
- Difficulty estimator

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 50 levels created
- [ ] All levels verified solvable
- [ ] Optimal moves documented
- [ ] Difficulty curve smooth
- [ ] Playtested and enjoyable

title: EPIC: Game Pipeline Infrastructure
description:
# EPIC: Game Pipeline Infrastructure

## Strategic Context

The game pipeline is the operational framework for continuously expanding the game library. Without systematic processes, adding new games becomes ad-hoc and quality inconsistent.

## Why This Matters

1. **Scalability:** Add games without reinventing process each time
2. **Quality Consistency:** Same standards for every game
3. **Contributor Friendly:** External contributors can follow process
4. **Time Savings:** Templates and automation reduce overhead
5. **Maintainability:** Standardized code is easier to maintain

## Pipeline Phases

```
DISCOVER ──> BUILD ──> TEST ──> DEPLOY

Find games    Scaffold &    QA, perf,    PR, review,
worth making  implement     playtest     release
```

## Phase 1: Discovery

### Source Channels

**Primary:**
- App Store / Play Store ads (fake gameplay clips)
- TikTok/Reels (viral puzzle game clips)
- Reddit r/mobilegaming, r/puzzles
- Itch.io game jams

**Secondary:**
- Player requests (GitHub issues)
- Direct competitor analysis
- Classic puzzle game adaptations

### Evaluation Criteria

Each potential game gets scored on:
- Technical feasibility (client-side only, no backend)
- Gameplay appeal (satisfying, clear, depth)
- Mobile-friendliness (touch controls, portrait)
- Shareability (GIF-worthy solutions)

### Prototyping Gate

Before full implementation, 1-2 day "fun validation":
- Core mechanic only
- 3 playable levels
- Basic touch controls
- Answer: "Is this fun?" Yes/No

## Phase 2: Build

### Scaffold Generator

`scripts/new-game.sh` creates standardized structure:
- index.html (game page)
- main.js (entry point)
- game.js (core logic)
- renderer.js (canvas rendering)
- input.js (touch/mouse handling)
- styles.css (game-specific)
- levels.json (level data)
- generator.js (procedural generation)
- assets/ (sprites, audio)

### Code Patterns

All games follow consistent patterns:
- State immutability (structuredClone)
- Event-driven input
- Animation queue system
- Shared color palette

## Phase 3: Test

### Test Suites

**Unit Tests (Vitest):**
- Game logic validation
- Generator determinism
- Solvability verification

**E2E Tests (Playwright):**
- Level completion flows
- Touch control verification
- Mobile viewport testing

### QA Checklist

44-item manual testing checklist covering:
- Core gameplay
- Controls
- History system
- Progression
- Visual polish
- Audio
- Responsive design
- Performance
- Edge cases

### Playtest Protocol

1. Internal playtest (developer)
2. Friend playtest (3-5 people)
3. Public beta (GitHub issue)

## Phase 4: Deploy

### CI/CD Pipeline

- Tests run on PR
- Preview deploy for PRs
- Production deploy on merge to main
- Bundle size budgets enforced

### PR Checklist

Required for new game PRs:
- All scaffold files present
- 20+ levels
- Unit tests passing
- E2E tests passing
- QA checklist completed
- Bundle under budget

### Release Process

1. Feature branch (feat/game-slug)
2. PR with checklist
3. Review
4. Merge to main
5. Auto-deploy
6. Social announcement

## Dependencies

**Requires:**
- Foundation complete

**Enables:**
- All future game additions
- Contributor onboarding

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Evaluation template exists
- [ ] Scaffold generator works
- [ ] All templates documented
- [ ] CI validates new games
- [ ] PR checklist enforced

## Estimated Effort

**Total:** 10-15 hours (infrastructure setup)
**Complexity:** Low-Medium
**Priority:** P2 (Enables scaling)

title: Create game scaffold generator script
description:
# Create Game Scaffold Generator

## Script: scripts/new-game.sh

Creates standardized directory structure for new games:
- index.html (from template)
- main.js (entry point)
- game.js (core logic stub)
- renderer.js (canvas stub)
- input.js (input handling stub)
- styles.css (game-specific)
- levels.json (empty)
- generator.js (stub)
- assets/ directories

## Usage
```bash
./scripts/new-game.sh "Color Match"
# Creates src/games/color-match/
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script creates all files
- [ ] Placeholders filled correctly
- [ ] Game name/slug substituted
- [ ] README with next steps

title: EPIC: Launch & Growth
description:
# EPIC: Launch & Growth

## Strategic Context

Launch is not just going live - it's the coordinated release of a polished product with marketing support. Growth continues post-launch with iteration based on real user data.

## Launch Checklist

### Pre-Launch
- [ ] All 4 games functional
- [ ] PWA installable
- [ ] Offline mode working
- [ ] All platform features live
- [ ] Performance targets met
- [ ] Accessibility audit passed
- [ ] Legal review (copyright, privacy)

### Launch Day
- [ ] Final smoke test
- [ ] Social announcement posts
- [ ] Submit to PWA directories
- [ ] Monitor error tracking
- [ ] Respond to initial feedback

### Post-Launch (Week 1)
- [ ] Fix critical bugs
- [ ] Analyze user behavior
- [ ] Identify friction points
- [ ] Plan first improvements

## Marketing Channels

### Organic
- Reddit (r/WebGames, r/IndieGaming)
- Hacker News
- Twitter/X gaming communities
- TikTok gameplay clips
- Product Hunt

### Content
- Share GIFs from games
- Daily challenge announcements
- Milestone celebrations
- Behind-the-scenes dev content

## Success Metrics

### Launch Week
| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Unique visitors | 1,000 |
| Game completions | 5,000 |
| PWA installs | 100 |
| Social shares | 50 |

### Month 1
| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| DAU | 200 |
| Retention D1 | 30% |
| Retention D7 | 15% |
| Games per session | 3 |

## Growth Levers

1. **Daily Challenges** - Reason to return
2. **Streaks** - Commitment device
3. **Share Cards** - Viral loops
4. **New Games** - Content freshness
5. **Word of Mouth** - Quality product

## Dependencies

**Requires:**
- All other epics complete

**Enables:**
- Ongoing growth
- Revenue (future)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Launch announcement posted
- [ ] No critical bugs first 48 hours
- [ ] Analytics tracking working
- [ ] Error monitoring active
- [ ] Feedback collection working

## Estimated Effort

**Total:** 10-15 hours (launch activities)
**Complexity:** Low
**Priority:** P3 (Final milestone)

title: Create launch announcement assets
description: Create social media posts, GIFs showcasing gameplay, screenshots for app directories.

title: Submit to PWA directories
description: Submit to: PWA.rocks, AppScope, PWA Store. Prepare required assets and descriptions.

title: EPIC: Polish & Optimization
description:
# EPIC: Polish & Optimization

## Strategic Context

Polish transforms a functional product into a delightful one. Optimization ensures it performs well on real devices. Both are essential for retention and word-of-mouth growth.

## Scope

### 1. Performance Optimization
- Bundle size reduction
- Load time optimization
- Animation smoothness
- Memory management

### 2. Accessibility
- Colorblind modes
- Reduced motion support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Keyboard navigation

### 3. Cross-Browser/Device Testing
- iOS Safari quirks
- Android Chrome
- Firefox mobile
- Older devices

### 4. Visual Polish
- Animation refinement
- Micro-interactions
- Loading states
- Error states

## Performance Targets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| First Contentful Paint | < 1.5s |
| Time to Interactive | < 2.5s |
| Total bundle (per game) | < 150KB gzipped |
| Animation framerate | 60fps |
| Lighthouse Score | > 90 |

## Accessibility Requirements

### Colorblind Support
- Pattern overlays (stripes, dots)
- High contrast mode
- Color-agnostic gameplay design

### Motion Sensitivity
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion
- Disable confetti/particles
- Reduce animation intensity

### Screen Readers
- Semantic HTML
- ARIA labels
- Focus management

## Cross-Platform Matrix

| Platform | Priority | Known Issues |
|----------|----------|--------------|
| iOS Safari | P1 | 100vh bug, audio autoplay |
| Android Chrome | P1 | Touch delay, viewport |
| iOS Chrome | P2 | Similar to Safari |
| Firefox Mobile | P2 | Rendering differences |
| Samsung Internet | P3 | Older engine |
| Desktop Chrome | P1 | Reference platform |
| Desktop Firefox | P2 | CSS grid quirks |
| Desktop Safari | P2 | Audio context issues |

## Dependencies

**Requires:**
- All games implemented
- Platform features complete

**Parallel With:**
- Zen mode
- Social sharing

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lighthouse PWA > 90
- [ ] Lighthouse Performance > 85
- [ ] Lighthouse Accessibility > 90
- [ ] Works on 3-year-old devices
- [ ] All colorblind modes functional
- [ ] Reduced motion respected
- [ ] No memory leaks (1hr session)

## Estimated Effort

**Total:** 20-25 hours
**Complexity:** Medium-High
**Priority:** P3 (Pre-launch)

title: Implement colorblind accessibility mode
description:
# Implement Colorblind Mode

## Mode Types
- Deuteranopia (red-green)
- Protanopia (red-green, different)
- Tritanopia (blue-yellow)

## Implementation
1. Pattern overlays (stripes, dots, circles)
2. Distinct shapes per color
3. High contrast option
4. Settings toggle

## Testing
- Use colorblind simulation tools
- Test with actual colorblind users if possible

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All game colors distinguishable
- [ ] Patterns render correctly
- [ ] Settings persist
- [ ] No performance impact

title: Performance audit and optimization
description:
# Performance Audit

## Targets
- First Contentful Paint: < 1.5s
- Time to Interactive: < 2.5s
- Bundle size per game: < 150KB gzip
- Animation framerate: 60fps constant

## Audit Areas
1. Bundle size analysis
2. Code splitting effectiveness
3. Image optimization
4. Animation performance
5. Memory usage (no leaks)

## Tools
- Lighthouse
- Chrome DevTools Performance tab
- Bundle analyzer
- Memory profiler

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All targets met
- [ ] No memory leaks in 1hr session
- [ ] Works on 3-year-old devices
- [ ] Lighthouse Performance > 85

title: Cross-browser testing pass
description: Test on: iOS Safari, Android Chrome, Firefox Mobile, Desktop browsers. Document and fix issues.

title: EPIC: Platform Features
description:
# EPIC: Platform Features (Cross-Game Systems)

## Strategic Context

Platform features transform four individual games into a cohesive gaming platform with retention mechanics. These systems create the "one more game" loop that keeps players returning.

## Feature Overview

### 1. Progressive Web App (PWA)
**Why:** Native-app feel without app store friction
- Install to home screen
- Offline gameplay
- Full-screen experience
- Background caching

### 2. Cross-Game Meta-Progression
**Why:** Progress in any game advances your profile
- Unified XP system
- Player levels with unlocks
- Cosmetic rewards
- "Completionist" motivation

### 3. Achievement System
**Why:** Clear goals drive engagement
- Per-game mastery badges
- Cross-game challenges
- Streak rewards
- Social bragging rights

### 4. Daily Challenge System
**Why:** Daily return motivation
- Same puzzle worldwide (seed-based)
- Streak tracking
- Bonus rewards
- FOMO for retention

## Detailed Specifications

### PWA Implementation

**manifest.json:**
```json
{
  "name": "Mobile Gaming",
  "short_name": "Games",
  "description": "Browser puzzle games that deliver what the ads promised",
  "start_url": "/",
  "display": "standalone",
  "orientation": "portrait",
  "background_color": "#0f0f23",
  "theme_color": "#00d4ff",
  "icons": [
    { "src": "/icons/icon-192.png", "sizes": "192x192", "type": "image/png", "purpose": "any maskable" },
    { "src": "/icons/icon-512.png", "sizes": "512x512", "type": "image/png", "purpose": "any maskable" }
  ],
  "screenshots": [
    { "src": "/screenshots/water-sort.png", "sizes": "390x844", "type": "image/png" }
  ],
  "categories": ["games", "puzzle"]
}
```

**Service Worker Strategy:**
```javascript
// Cache-first for static assets
// Network-first for level data (allows updates)
// Stale-while-revalidate for icons

const CACHE_VERSION = 'v1';
const STATIC_CACHE = `static-${CACHE_VERSION}`;
const DYNAMIC_CACHE = `dynamic-${CACHE_VERSION}`;

const STATIC_ASSETS = [
  '/',
  '/index.html',
  '/shared/meta.js',
  '/shared/storage.js',
  // ... all core files
  '/games/water-sort/index.html',
  '/games/water-sort/game.js',
  // ... all game files
];
```

**Install Prompt UX:**
- Show after 3rd session (not first visit)
- Dismissable, remembers preference
- Re-prompt after 30 days if dismissed

### Meta-Progression System

**Data Model:**
```javascript
{
  version: 1,  // Schema version for migrations
  xp: 4250,
  level: 12,
  nextLevelXP: 5000,
  gamesPlayed: {
    'water-sort': { 
      played: 45, 
      completed: 42,
      stars: 87, 
      bestStreak: 7,
      perfectClears: 12  // 3-star completions
    },
    // ... other games
  },
  achievements: ['first_win', 'streak_7', 'master_sorter'],
  unlockedCosmetics: ['theme_dark', 'tube_neon', 'car_gold'],
  dailyStreak: 7,
  longestStreak: 14,
  lastDailyDate: '2026-03-05',
  settings: {
    soundEnabled: true,
    hapticEnabled: true,
    reducedMotion: false,
    zenMode: false,
    colorblindMode: 'none'
  },
  createdAt: '2026-01-15T10:30:00Z',
  lastPlayedAt: '2026-03-05T14:22:00Z'
}
```

**XP Formula:**
```javascript
const XP_AWARDS = {
  LEVEL_COMPLETE: 10,
  STAR_BONUS: 5,        // Per star (max 15 for 3 stars)
  DAILY_COMPLETE: 25,
  STREAK_BONUS: (days) => days * 5,  // 7-day streak = 35 bonus
  ACHIEVEMENT: (tier) => ({ bronze: 50, silver: 100, gold: 200 })[tier]
};

const LEVEL_THRESHOLDS = [
  0, 100, 250, 500, 800, 1200, 1700, 2300, 3000, 4000,
  5000, 6500, 8000, 10000, 12500, 15000, 18000, 22000, 27000, 33000,
  40000, 50000, 62000, 76000, 92000, 110000, 130000, 152000, 176000, 200000
];
```

**Unlockables:**
| Level | Unlock | Type |
|-------|--------|------|
| 2 | Dark Theme | Theme |
| 5 | Neon Tubes | Water Sort Skin |
| 8 | Metallic Cars | Parking Skin |
| 10 | Confetti Effects | Celebration |
| 15 | Custom Backgrounds | Theme |
| 20 | Animated Themes | Theme |
| 25 | Gold Everything | Premium Skin |
| 30 | Achievement Frames | Profile |

### Achievement System

**Categories:**

**Progress Achievements:**
| ID | Name | Criteria | XP |
|----|------|----------|-----|
| first_win | First Steps | Complete any level | 50 |
| dedicated | Dedicated | Complete 50 levels | 100 |
| centurion | Centurion | Complete 100 levels | 200 |
| marathoner | Marathoner | Complete 500 levels | 500 |

**Streak Achievements:**
| ID | Name | Criteria | XP |
|----|------|----------|-----|
| streak_3 | Getting Started | 3-day streak | 50 |
| streak_7 | Weekly Warrior | 7-day streak | 100 |
| streak_30 | Monthly Master | 30-day streak | 200 |
| streak_100 | Legendary | 100-day streak | 500 |

**Mastery Achievements (per game):**
| ID Pattern | Name | Criteria | XP |
|------------|------|----------|-----|
| {game}_novice | Novice | 25 stars | 50 |
| {game}_adept | Adept | 50 stars | 100 |
| {game}_master | Master | 100 stars | 200 |
| {game}_legend | Legend | 200 stars | 500 |

**Challenge Achievements:**
| ID | Name | Criteria | XP |
|----|------|----------|-----|
| speedster | Speedster | Complete any level < 5 seconds | 100 |
| perfectionist | Perfectionist | 10 perfect (3-star) clears in a row | 150 |
| no_undo | Confident | Complete 10 levels without undo | 100 |
| explorer | Explorer | Play all games | 50 |

### Daily Challenge System

**Seed Algorithm:**
```javascript
export function getDailySeed() {
  const today = new Date();
  const dateStr = today.toISOString().slice(0, 10); // "2026-03-05"
  return hashString(dateStr + 'mobile-gaming-daily-v1');
}

export function getDailyChallenge(game) {
  const baseSeed = getDailySeed();
  const gameSeed = hashString(baseSeed + game);
  return generators[game].generate(gameSeed, { difficulty: 'medium' });
}

function hashString(str) {
  let hash = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < str.length; i++) {
    hash = ((hash << 5) - hash) + str.charCodeAt(i);
    hash |= 0;
  }
  return Math.abs(hash);
}
```

**Daily Challenge Storage:**
```javascript
{
  [`daily_${date}_${game}`]: {
    completed: true,
    moves: 12,
    time: 45,
    stars: 2,
    attempts: 1,
    completedAt: '2026-03-05T14:22:00Z'
  }
}
```

**Streak Logic:**
```javascript
function updateStreak(profile) {
  const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);
  const yesterday = new Date(Date.now() - 86400000).toISOString().slice(0, 10);
  
  if (profile.lastDailyDate === today) {
    return; // Already counted today
  }
  
  if (profile.lastDailyDate === yesterday) {
    profile.dailyStreak++;
  } else {
    profile.dailyStreak = 1; // Streak broken, restart
  }
  
  profile.longestStreak = Math.max(profile.longestStreak, profile.dailyStreak);
  profile.lastDailyDate = today;
}
```

## Integration Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        Profile UI                            │
│  (Level display, XP bar, achievement grid, streak counter)  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                       shared/meta.js                         │
│  addXP(), getLevel(), unlockAchievement(), checkAchievements │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     shared/storage.js                        │
│  save(), load(), migrate(), export(), import()               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                       localStorage                           │
│           (with IndexedDB fallback for large data)          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Dependencies

**Requires:**
- Foundation (storage module)

**Enables:**
- All games use these systems
- Social sharing uses achievement data
- Zen mode modifies XP awards

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] PWA installable on iOS and Android
- [ ] Works fully offline after install
- [ ] XP awards correctly for all actions
- [ ] Level-up notification appears
- [ ] Achievements unlock and notify
- [ ] Daily challenge same for all users
- [ ] Streak tracks correctly across days
- [ ] Data persists across sessions
- [ ] Data survives browser cache clear (warning)

## Estimated Effort

**Total:** 25-30 hours
**Complexity:** Medium
**Priority:** P1 (Enables retention)

title: Implement achievement system
Create profile page UI
description: Profile page showing: level, XP, achievements grid, game stats, streak calendar, share button.

title: EPIC: Parking Escape Game
description:
# EPIC: Parking Escape (Rush Hour Clone)

## Strategic Context

Parking Escape is the second most recognizable "fake ad" game mechanic. The sliding puzzle format offers a different cognitive challenge from Water Sort - spatial reasoning vs. color matching.

## Why This Game?

1. **High Recognition:** "Rush Hour" mechanics appear in countless mobile ads
2. **Spatial Reasoning:** Complements Water Sort's color-matching logic
3. **Clear Win State:** Target car exits = obvious satisfaction
4. **Drag Controls:** Validates drag-based input handling (used by Bus Jam too)
5. **Well-Studied:** Optimal solvers exist, level generation is solved problem

## Game Mechanics

### State Representation
```javascript
{
  gridSize: 6,  // 6x6 grid
  cars: [
    { id: "target", x: 0, y: 2, length: 2, orientation: "horizontal", color: "red" },
    { id: "car1", x: 2, y: 0, length: 2, orientation: "vertical", color: "blue" },
    { id: "truck1", x: 0, y: 4, length: 3, orientation: "horizontal", color: "yellow" }
  ],
  exit: { x: 6, y: 2 },  // Right edge, row 2
  moves: 0
}
```

### Core Rules

**Vehicle Properties:**
- Length: 2 (car) or 3 (truck)
- Orientation: horizontal or vertical
- Can only slide along orientation axis
- Cannot overlap other vehicles
- Cannot leave grid (except target through exit)

**Valid Move:**
- Slide vehicle 1+ cells along its axis
- No collision with other vehicles
- Within grid bounds

**Win Condition:**
- Target car (red, horizontal, row 2) reaches x=5
- Animation: car drives through exit

### Visual Design

**Top-Down View:**
```
╭─────────╮
│  ◯   ◯  │   ← Car (2 cells)
╰─────────╯

╭───┬─────────╮
│ ◯◯│  CARGO  │   ← Truck (3 cells)
╰───┴─────────╯
```

**Color Palette:**
```css
--car-red: #E63946;     /* Target - always red */
--car-blue: #457B9D;
--car-green: #2A9D8F;
--car-yellow: #E9C46A;
--car-purple: #9D4EDD;
--car-orange: #F77F00;

--grid-bg: #264653;
--grid-line: rgba(255,255,255,0.2);
--exit-glow: #00FF88;
```

**Animations:**
| Action | Duration | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| Drag | Continuous | Car follows finger with shadow growth |
| Snap | 200ms | Overshoot ease to grid position |
| Collision | 150ms | Flash blocked car red |
| Win | 1000ms | Target accelerates through exit, others fade |

### Level Generation

**Algorithm (from research):**
1. Place target car at random starting x on exit row
2. Randomly place blocking cars
3. Use BFS to find minimum solution moves
4. Filter: must require 10+ moves (too easy otherwise)
5. Verify unique solution path (optional for variety)

**Difficulty Tiers:**
- Easy: 10-20 move solution
- Medium: 21-35 move solution
- Hard: 36-50 move solution
- Expert: 50+ move solution

## Integration Points

**Shared Systems:**
- All standard shared modules
- Drag input handling (reused by Bus Jam)

**Unique Considerations:**
- Grid-based snapping logic
- Collision detection
- Pathfinding for hint system

## Dependencies

**Requires:**
- Foundation complete
- Water Sort validates shared systems

**Enables:**
- Bus Jam (similar grid/drag mechanics)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 40 handcrafted levels
- [ ] Procedural generator working
- [ ] Drag controls feel smooth
- [ ] Grid snapping accurate
- [ ] Collision feedback clear
- [ ] Minimum moves displayed
- [ ] Star rating based on efficiency

## Estimated Effort

**Total:** 25-30 hours
**Complexity:** Medium
**Priority:** P2 (After Water Sort)

## State Representation

title: EPIC: Foundation & Infrastructure
description:
# EPIC: Foundation & Infrastructure

## Strategic Context

This epic establishes the technical foundation for the entire Mobile Gaming platform. Every subsequent feature, game, and enhancement depends on these core systems being robust, well-documented, and performant.

## Business Rationale

**Why Foundation First:**
- A solid foundation prevents costly rework later
- Shared systems (storage, XP, PWA) must exist before games can use them
- CI/CD pipeline enables rapid iteration once established
- PWA capability is critical for mobile-first user retention

**Success Metrics:**
- Build time < 30s
- Lighthouse PWA score > 90
- Time to first interactive < 2.5s
- 100% offline capability for gameplay

## Technical Scope

### 1. Build System (Vite)
- Module bundling with tree-shaking
- Hot module replacement for development
- Code splitting per game (lazy loading)
- Asset optimization (images, fonts)

### 2. Project Structure
```
mobile-gaming/
├── src/
│   ├── index.html           # Landing/directory page
│   ├── shared/              # Cross-game systems
│   │   ├── meta.js          # XP, levels, unlocks
│   │   ├── storage.js       # localStorage abstraction
│   │   ├── daily.js         # Daily challenge seeding
│   │   ├── audio.js         # Sound manager
│   │   ├── history.js       # Undo/redo system
│   │   ├── rng.js           # Seeded PRNG
│   │   └── colors.js        # Shared color palette
│   ├── games/               # Individual games
│   └── styles/              # Global CSS
├── public/
│   ├── icons/               # PWA icons
│   ├── audio/               # Sound effects
│   └── manifest.json        # PWA manifest
├── scripts/                 # Build/dev scripts
├── docs/                    # Documentation
└── tests/                   # Test suites
```

### 3. PWA Implementation
- Service worker with cache-first strategy
- Offline-first architecture
- Install prompt handling
- Background sync for future analytics

### 4. CI/CD Pipeline
- GitHub Actions workflow
- Preview deploys on PRs
- Bundle size budgets
- Automated testing gates

## Dependencies

**External:**
- Cloudflare Pages account configured (DONE)
- GitHub repository (DONE - jedarden/mobile-gaming)
- GitHub Actions secrets (DONE - CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN, CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID)

**Produces (enables):**
- All game implementations depend on shared systems
- All platform features depend on storage/meta systems
- All deploys depend on CI/CD pipeline

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| PWA complexity | Medium | High | Follow Workbox patterns |
| Cache invalidation bugs | Medium | High | Version all cache names |
| Build tool issues | Low | Medium | Pin Vite version |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm run dev` starts local development server
- [ ] `npm run build` produces optimized production bundle
- [ ] `npm run test` runs all unit tests
- [ ] PWA installable on mobile devices
- [ ] Works fully offline after first load
- [ ] CI/CD deploys to Cloudflare Pages on push to main
- [ ] Preview URLs generated for PRs

## Estimated Effort

**Total:** 15-20 hours
**Complexity:** Medium
**Priority:** P1 (Critical Path)

## Related Documentation

- docs/implementation.md (comprehensive spec)
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml (CI/CD config)

title: Set up Vite build system with dev server
description:
# Set up Vite Build System

## Context
Vite is chosen as the build tool for its:
- Fast HMR (Hot Module Replacement) for development
- Efficient production builds with tree-shaking
- Native ES modules support
- Zero-config TypeScript/JSX support if needed later

## Technical Requirements

### vite.config.js
```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import { resolve } from 'path';

export default defineConfig({
  root: 'src',
  publicDir: '../public',
  build: {
    outDir: '../dist',
    emptyOutDir: true,
    rollupOptions: {
      input: {
        main: resolve(__dirname, 'src/index.html'),
        'water-sort': resolve(__dirname, 'src/games/water-sort/index.html'),
        'parking-escape': resolve(__dirname, 'src/games/parking-escape/index.html'),
        'bus-jam': resolve(__dirname, 'src/games/bus-jam/index.html'),
        'pull-the-pin': resolve(__dirname, 'src/games/pull-the-pin/index.html'),
      }
    }
  },
  server: {
    port: 3000,
    open: true
  }
});
```

### package.json scripts
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "test": "vitest",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "lint": "eslint src/"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] npm run dev starts dev server on port 3000
- [ ] HMR works for JS/CSS changes
- [ ] npm run build produces optimized output
- [ ] dist/ contains all game entry points
- [ ] Source maps generated for debugging

## Why This Matters
The build system is the foundation for all development work. A well-configured Vite setup enables:
- Rapid iteration during development
- Code splitting for performance
- Consistent builds across environments

title: Set up CI/CD with GitHub Actions
description:
# Set up CI/CD Pipeline

## Context
CI/CD enables:
- Automated testing on every PR
- Preview deployments for review
- Consistent production deploys
- Quality gates before merge

## Workflow Design

### .github/workflows/deploy.yml
```yaml
name: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm run test
      - run: npm run test:e2e

  build:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - name: Check bundle size
        run: |
          MAX_SIZE=500000  # 500KB
          ACTUAL=$(du -sb dist | cut -f1)
          if [ "$ACTUAL" -gt "$MAX_SIZE" ]; then
            echo "Bundle too large: $ACTUAL > $MAX_SIZE"
            exit 1
          fi
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/
      - uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: pages deploy dist --project-name=mobile-gaming --branch=${{ github.head_ref }}
      - name: Comment preview URL
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: '🎮 Preview: https://${{ github.head_ref }}.mobile-gaming.pages.dev'
            })

  deploy-production:
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/
      - uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: pages deploy dist --project-name=mobile-gaming
```

## Required Secrets
- CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN (already configured)
- CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID (already configured)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests run on every PR
- [ ] Lint errors fail the build
- [ ] Bundle size check enforced
- [ ] Preview deploys on PRs
- [ ] Production deploys on main push
- [ ] Preview URL commented on PR

title: Create project directory structure
description:
# Create Project Directory Structure

## Context
A well-organized directory structure enables:
- Clear separation of concerns
- Easy navigation for developers
- Predictable file locations
- Scalability for new games

## Target Structure
```
mobile-gaming/
├── src/
│   ├── index.html           # Landing/directory page
│   ├── profile/             # Player profile page
│   │   └── index.html
│   ├── shared/              # Cross-game modules
│   │   ├── meta.js          # XP, levels, unlocks
│   │   ├── storage.js       # localStorage abstraction
│   │   ├── daily.js         # Daily challenge seeding
│   │   ├── audio.js         # Sound manager
│   │   ├── history.js       # Undo/redo system
│   │   ├── rng.js           # Seeded PRNG
│   │   ├── colors.js        # Shared color palette
│   │   └── share.js         # Sharing utilities
│   ├── styles/              # Global CSS
│   │   ├── reset.css        # CSS reset
│   │   ├── variables.css    # CSS custom properties
│   │   ├── game-shell.css   # Shared game layout
│   │   └── components.css   # Reusable components
│   └── games/               # Individual games
│       ├── water-sort/
│       ├── parking-escape/
│       ├── bus-jam/
│       └── pull-the-pin/
├── public/
│   ├── icons/               # PWA icons (192, 512)
│   ├── audio/               # Sound effects
│   │   ├── sfx/             # Game sounds
│   │   └── ambient/         # Zen mode background
│   ├── manifest.json        # PWA manifest
│   └── sw.js                # Service worker
├── scripts/                 # Build/dev scripts
│   └── new-game.sh          # Game scaffold generator
├── tests/                   # Test suites
│   ├── unit/                # Vitest unit tests
│   └── e2e/                 # Playwright e2e tests
├── docs/                    # Documentation
│   ├── implementation.md
│   └── templates/           # QA checklists, etc.
└── .beads/                  # Task tracking
```

## Actions
1. Create all directories
2. Add .gitkeep to empty directories
3. Create placeholder files for shared modules

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All directories exist
- [ ] Placeholder files in place
- [ ] No empty directories in git

## Rationale
This structure follows conventions from successful web game projects and enables
the modular architecture described in the implementation guide.

title: Implement shared storage module (localStorage abstraction)
Implement PWA manifest and service worker
Implement shared meta-progression module (XP, levels, unlocks)
Implement seeded PRNG (Mulberry32)
Implement game history module (undo/redo)
Implement daily challenge seeding module
Create landing page (game directory)
description:
# Create Landing Page ## Context The landing page is the first impression. It should: - Showcase all available games - Display player progress - Promote daily challenges - Enable PWA install ## Design ### Layout (Mobile Portrait) ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ 🎮 Mobile Gaming [Profile] │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ 📅 Daily Challenge │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Water Sort - Solve Today! │ │ │ │ 🔥 7 day streak │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ 🎯 Games │ │ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ │ │ │ Water │ │ Parking │ │ │ │ Sort │ │ Escape │ │ │ │ ⭐ 45 │ │ ⭐ 23 │ │ │ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ │ │ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ │ │ │ Bus │ │ Pull the │ │ │ │ Jam │ │ Pin │ │ │ │ ⭐ 12 │ │ ⭐ 8 │ │ │ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ │ │ │ │ ─────────────────────────────── │ │ Level 12 ● 4,250 XP │ │ ████████████░░░░ 850 to next │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### HTML Structure ```html Mobile Gaming - Puzzle Games

🎮 Mobile Gaming

Lv.1

📅 Daily Challenge

Water Sort Solve Today! 🔥 0 day streak

🎯 Games

💧

Water Sort

⭐ 0
Level 1 • 0 XP
100 to next level

Install for offline play!

``` ### JavaScript (landing.js) ```javascript import { getProfile, calculateLevel, getXPForNextLevel } from '/shared/meta.js'; import { getStreakInfo, isDailyCompleted } from '/shared/daily.js'; function updateUI() { const profile = getProfile(); const streak = getStreakInfo(); // Update level document.getElementById('player-level').textContent = profile.level; document.getElementById('current-level').textContent = profile.level; document.getElementById('current-xp').textContent = profile.xp.toLocaleString(); // Update XP bar const currentLevelXP = LEVEL_THRESHOLDS[profile.level - 1] || 0; const nextLevelXP = getXPForNextLevel(profile.xp); const progress = nextLevelXP ? ((profile.xp - currentLevelXP) / (nextLevelXP - currentLevelXP)) * 100 : 100; document.querySelector('.xp-fill').style.width = `${progress}%`; document.getElementById('xp-needed').textContent = nextLevelXP ? (nextLevelXP - profile.xp).toLocaleString() : '∞'; // Update streak document.getElementById('streak-count').textContent = streak.current; // Update game cards Object.entries(profile.gamesPlayed || {}).forEach(([game, data]) => { const card = document.querySelector(`[data-game="${game}"] .star-count`); if (card) card.textContent = data.stars; }); } // Run on load updateUI(); ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All games displayed as cards - [ ] Daily challenge section shows current game - [ ] Streak count displays correctly - [ ] XP bar reflects current progress - [ ] Level badge updates - [ ] Responsive on mobile/desktop - [ ] Install banner appears appropriately

title: EPIC: Bus Jam Game
description:
# EPIC: Bus Jam (Passenger Sorting Puzzle)

## Strategic Context

Bus Jam represents a newer genre of puzzle ads featuring color-matched passengers loading onto buses. This is currently trending in mobile ad content and offers more dynamic visual interest than static sliding puzzles.

## Why This Game?

1. **Trending Now:** "Jam" style games are currently dominating fake gameplay ads
2. **Satisfying Animation:** Passengers boarding creates delightful micro-animations
3. **Different Logic:** Route planning + color matching hybrid
4. **Audio Potential:** Bus honks, passenger boarding sounds are memorable
5. **Higher Complexity:** Supports our adaptive difficulty system well

## Game Mechanics

### State Representation
```javascript
{
  grid: { cols: 5, rows: 7 },
  buses: [
    { 
      id: "bus1", 
      x: 1, y: 3, 
      color: "red", 
      passengers: 0, 
      capacity: 4,
      direction: "right"  // Facing direction
    }
  ],
  stops: [
    { x: 0, y: 2, color: "red", waiting: 4 }  // 4 red passengers waiting
  ],
  exits: [{ x: 5, y: 3 }],  // Right edge
  roads: [[1,3], [2,3], [3,3], [4,3]],  // Drivable cells
  moves: 0
}
```

### Core Rules

**Bus Behavior:**
- Buses can only move on road cells
- Buses move one cell at a time (path planning)
- Buses cannot pass through other buses
- At matching color stop: passengers auto-board
- Full bus can exit through exit point

**Stop Behavior:**
- Stops have a color and a queue of passengers
- When bus of same color adjacent: 1 passenger boards per tick
- Boarding animation: passenger hops arc into bus
- Stop becomes empty when all passengers boarded

**Win Condition:**
- All passengers delivered (all buses exited or all stops empty)
- Fail: Gridlock (no valid moves) - but we avoid fail states for zen

**Move Counting:**
- Each bus move = 1 move
- Boarding is free (encourages proper routing)

### Visual Design

**Isometric-ish Top-Down View:**
```
  🚌 ← Bus (2x1 cells visual, 1 cell collision)
  
  🚏 ← Stop with colored passengers
```

**Color Palette:**
```css
--bus-red: #FF6B6B;
--bus-blue: #4DABF7;
--bus-green: #69DB7C;
--bus-yellow: #FFD93D;

--road: #4A4A5A;
--road-marking: #FFFFFF;
--grass: #2D5A27;
--stop-base: #888888;
```

**Passenger Design:**
- Simple stick figures with colored shirts
- Idle animation: slight bob
- Boarding animation: jump arc with anticipation

**Animations:**
| Action | Duration | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| Bus select | 150ms | Slight scale up, highlight |
| Bus move | 400ms/cell | Smooth translation |
| Passenger board | 300ms each | Jump arc into bus door |
| Bus full indicator | - | Capacity dots fill up |
| Bus exit | 600ms | Accelerate off screen + honk |
| Level complete | 1000ms | All buses honk sequence |

### Level Generation

**Approach:**
1. Generate road layout (connected paths)
2. Place exits at edges
3. Place stops along roads with passenger counts
4. Place buses with matching total capacity to passengers
5. Verify solvable (simulation with BFS)

**Complexity Factors:**
- Number of bus colors
- Road layout complexity
- Blocking positions
- Required sequencing

## Integration Points

**Shared Systems:**
- Standard modules
- More complex pathfinding than Parking Escape
- Audio system heavily used (boarding, honks)

**Unique Systems:**
- Road network graph
- Passenger queue simulation
- Multi-step move planning

## Zen Mode Considerations

Bus Jam works well in zen mode:
- Remove any timer
- Slower, more satisfying boarding animations
- No fail state for gridlock (provide hint/reset)
- Softer honk sounds

## Dependencies

**Requires:**
- Foundation complete
- Parking Escape (validates grid/drag patterns)

**Parallel With:**
- Pull the Pin (independent mechanics)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 30 handcrafted levels
- [ ] Route visualization when planning move
- [ ] Passenger boarding animation smooth
- [ ] Sound effects enhance experience
- [ ] No softlocks (always hint available)
- [ ] Performance stable with 8+ buses

## Estimated Effort

**Total:** 30-35 hours
**Complexity:** Medium-High
**Priority:** P2 (After Parking Escape)

title: Implement Bus Jam core game logic
description:
# Implement Bus Jam Core Logic

## State Representation
```javascript
{
  grid: { cols: 5, rows: 7 },
  buses: [
    { id: "bus1", x: 1, y: 3, color: "red", passengers: 0, capacity: 4 }
  ],
  stops: [
    { x: 0, y: 2, color: "red", waiting: 4 }
  ],
  exits: [{ x: 5, y: 3 }],
  roads: [[1,3], [2,3], [3,3]],
  moves: 0
}
```

## Core Rules
- Buses move on roads only
- Color-matched stops board passengers
- Full buses can exit
- Win when all passengers delivered

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pathfinding works
- [ ] Boarding logic correct
- [ ] Exit detection works
- [ ] Gridlock detection

title: EPIC: Zen Mode
Implement zen mode toggle and visual changes
description:
# Implement Zen Mode

## Visual Changes
- Softer color palette
- Slower animations
- Hide scores/timers
- Minimal UI

## Settings
- Toggle in settings menu
- Ambient audio selection
- Volume control

## CSS Approach
```css
.zen-mode {
  --liquid-red: #D4A5A5;
  --animation-speed: 1.5;
}

.zen-mode .move-counter,
.zen-mode .star-rating {
  display: none;
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toggle works
- [ ] Visual changes apply
- [ ] Scores hidden
- [ ] Animations slower
- [ ] Persists across sessions

title: Implement ambient audio system
description:
# Implement Ambient Audio

## Audio Tracks
- Rain
- Soft piano
- Nature sounds
- Lo-fi beats
- Ocean waves
- Silence (SFX only)

## Features
- Looping playback
- Smooth crossfade
- Volume control
- Track selection

## Implementation
```javascript
class AmbientAudio {
  async play(track) {
    await this.fadeOut(500);
    this.audio.src = this.tracks[track];
    await this.fadeIn(1000);
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All tracks play
- [ ] Crossfade smooth
- [ ] Volume persists
- [ ] No audio glitches
